items.title: SkillComposer: Learning to Evolve Agent Skills for Specification and Generalization
items.abstract: Agent skills, which consist of reusable strategies that guide agent reasoning and action, have shown strong potential for improving model capability at inference time. However, current skill construction methods treat the problem as one-shot extraction, overlooking a fundamental tension: a skill tailored to the specific task fails to transfer, while the abstracted skill often provides insufficient guidance. We attribute this fragility to the absence of explicit mechanisms for skill specification and generalization. To address this gap, we introduce SkillComposer, a framework that decomposes skill construction into three learnable operations: create, improve, and merge. Trained via systematic rejection sampling recipe, SkillComposer enables language models to self-evolve skills at inference time and supports three deployment modes: offline for building generalized libraries, online for task-specific refinement, and hybrid for combining both. Comprehensive experiments on $τ^2$-Bench, LiveCodeBench v6, and AppWorld show that SkillComposer consistently outperforms baselines. Our SkillComposer-4B improves a 27B executor by up to +4.5 on agent tasks and +3.4 on code tasks, while generalizing across domains and task types unseen during training. Analysis reveals that merge and improve address orthogonal quality dimensions and that skill composition is a transferable meta-ability, providing a practical recipe for skill-augmented inference.
items.llm_evidence_en: learning to evolve agent skills via create, improve, and merge operations
items.llm_evidence_cn: 通过创建、改进和合并操作学习演化智能体技能
items.llm_evidence: 通过创建、改进和合并操作学习演化智能体技能
items.canonical_evidence: 通过创建、改进和合并操作学习演化智能体技能
items.llm_tldr_en: SkillComposer decomposes skill construction into three learnable operations (create, improve, merge), trained via rejection sampling, enabling agents to evolve reusable skills that balance specificity and transferability.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 现有技能构建方法采用一次性提取，要么过于特化导致不迁移，要么过于抽象导致指导不足。本文提出SkillComposer，将技能构建分解为创建、改进和合并三个可学习操作，并通过拒绝采样训练。实验表明该方法能生成更有效、更通用的技能，显著提升智能体在多种任务上的表现。
items.llm_tldr: 现有技能构建方法采用一次性提取，要么过于特化导致不迁移，要么过于抽象导致指导不足。本文提出SkillComposer，将技能构建分解为创建、改进和合并三个可学习操作，并通过拒绝采样训练。实验表明该方法能生成更有效、更通用的技能，显著提升智能体在多种任务上的表现。

items.title: Retrospective Harness Optimization: Improving LLM Agents via Self-Preference over Trajectory Rollouts
items.abstract: AI agents rely on a harness of skills, tools, and workflows to solve complex problems. Continually improving this harness is essential for adapting to new tasks. However, existing optimization methods typically require ground-truth validation sets, yet such labeled data is difficult to acquire in practical deployment settings. To address this problem, we introduce Retrospective Harness Optimization (RHO), a self-supervised method that optimizes the agent harness using only past trajectories. Specifically, RHO selects a diverse coreset of challenging tasks from past trajectories and re-solves them in parallel. The agent analyzes these rollouts using self-validation and self-consistency, then generates candidate harness updates and selects the most effective one by its own pairwise self-preference. We evaluate RHO across three diverse domains, spanning software engineering, technical work, and knowledge work. Notably, a single optimization round improves the pass rate on SWE-Bench Pro from 59% to 78% without any external grading. Furthermore, our analysis demonstrates that RHO effectively targets prior failure modes. As a result, the optimized harness alters the agent's behavior patterns and sustains higher accuracy during long-horizon sessions.
items.llm_evidence_en: Self-supervised harness optimization via retrospective rollouts for self-improvement
items.llm_evidence_cn: 通过回顾轨迹的自监督优化实现自我改进
items.llm_evidence: 通过回顾轨迹的自监督优化实现自我改进
items.canonical_evidence: 通过回顾轨迹的自监督优化实现自我改进
items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes RHO, a self-supervised method that improves LLM agent harness by re-solving past tasks and using self-consistency for optimization.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 针对智能体优化需要人工标注验证集的问题，提出回顾式自监督方法RHO，从历史轨迹中选择多样化困难任务并重新求解，利用自验证和自一致性生成候选更新，无需真实标签即可持续改进智能体的技能、工具和工作流，实现自我提升。
items.llm_tldr: 针对智能体优化需要人工标注验证集的问题，提出回顾式自监督方法RHO，从历史轨迹中选择多样化困难任务并重新求解，利用自验证和自一致性生成候选更新，无需真实标签即可持续改进智能体的技能、工具和工作流，实现自我提升。
